title: PromotionGate operator-review-required path: end-to-end + Goodhart-pause-unpause scenario
goal: End-to-end test of the PromotionGate operator-review-required happy path AND the Goodhart-pause / operator-unpause scenario. Proves that (a) an Accept iteration does NOT mutate authority until PromotionGate returns Approved and (b) a Goodhart-triggered pause persists across scheduler invocations until the operator unpauses via IPC.
unblock_conditions: MT-152 (promotion floor), MT-153 (Goodhart sentinel), MT-154 (PromotionGate adapter), MT-155 (loop IPC), MT-156 (scheduler).
implementation_notes: Test scenario 1 (operator-review required path): (1) construct LoopCore + scheduler + mock PromotionGate that holds tickets in Pending state; (2) run one iteration with editable-surface proposal; (3) iteration reaches AcceptReject stage and submits to gate; (4) assert iteration status is PendingReview; (5) assert apply_proposal was NOT called on the editable surface (mutation blocked); (6) invoke list_pending_reviews IPC (MT-155), assert one PromotionRequest returned; (7) invoke approve_promotion IPC with rationale 'AC-test approval'; (8) assert apply_proposal NOW called on editable surface; (9) assert FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION emitted with Approved variant. Test scenario 2 (Goodhart pause + operator unpause): (1) seed scheduler with history of 3 prior accepted iterations whose dev/holdout gaps were 0.05, 0.10, 0.15 (monotonically widening); (2) run iteration 4 which evaluates a trial that produces gap 0.20 (4th widening); (3) assert GoodhartSentinel emits Pause with PauseReason::MonotonicGapWidening; (4) assert scheduler.try_schedule_next() returns Skip with GoodhartPause reason; (5) invoke unpause IPC (MT-155) with operator rationale 'reviewed and accepted Goodhart risk'; (6) assert subsequent scheduler.try_schedule_next() returns Schedule; (7) assert FR-EVT-LOOP-UNPAUSE emitted. Test scenario 3 (rejection path): (1) run iteration with editable-surface proposal; (2) gate returns Rejected; (3) assert iteration records rejection as memory (so future iterations don't repropose); (4) assert apply_proposal NOT called.
risk_if_missed: Without PromotionGate + Goodhart-pause end-to-end coverage the operator-review invariant could be bypassed at the integration seam; pause persistence across scheduler invocations is not provably enforced.
validator_focus: Mutation strictly blocked until Approved. Pause persists across scheduler invocations. Unpause requires operator IPC. All FR events asserted exactly once.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: apply_proposal is strictly gated by PromotionStatus::Approved; Pending or Rejected paths assert no mutation.
Goodhart pause persists across scheduler invocations and only operator IPC clears it.
Rejection is recorded as memory so future iterations cannot loop on the same rejected proposal.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: promotion_gate_pause_e2e_tests.rs ships 20 tests covering scenario 1 (operator-review required path), scenario 2 (Goodhart pause / operator unpause), scenario 3 (rejection path). Adversarial coverage includes: forged ticket id returns UnknownTicket; approval-without-pending returns UnknownIteration; double-approval second attempt fails; goodhart-takes-priority-over-pending at scheduler; both-pause-and-pending prefers pause. FR-EVT-LOOP-PAUSE / FR-EVT-LOOP-UNPAUSE / FR-EVT-PROMOTION-DECISION / FR-EVT-GOODHART-PAUSE constants verified. CONCERN: the test drives surface.snapshot() and surface.apply_proposal() MANUALLY rather than through LoopCore.run_one_iteration. The 'apply_proposal NOT called while Pending' assertion is true because the test simply hasn't called apply_proposal yet; it does NOT prove that production LoopCore actually blocks apply during a Pending review. Combined with the MT-154 ticket-binding gap and MT-155 IPC Pending-bypass, the end-to-end gate enforcement remains structurally questionable even though the unit-test invariants pass.
